rulings.Case Title: KIM BELLINO VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 4, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT LENARD RITZMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication on the harassment cause of action. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether the ¿black Twinkie¿ comment occurred on December 5, 2007. If it did, then Plaintiff can rely on the continuing violation doctrine and Defendant¿s prior on-going conduct is sufficient to create a triable issue of fact as to whether the comments were isolated and sporadic and constituted harassment. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: KIM BELLINO VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 4, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court grants Defendant UPS¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the Discrimination cause of action, the Failure to prevent Discrimination cause of action and Retaliation cause of action. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate any adverse employment conduct or animus. The only adverse employment conduct that Plaintiff complains about was when she was moved from the Center Manager position in Fremont to the Health and Safety Supervisor position. However, at pages 172-175 of her deposition, Plaintiff admits that she was the one that brought up the idea of moving because of the commute hardship. It appears she had a choice of remaining in her current position or transferring and chose the transfer. The Court also grants summary adjudication as to the punitive damages claim. CC §3294(b) provides that an employer shall not be liable for punitive damages based upon acts of an employee unless the employer had advanced knowledge of the unfitness of the employee and employed him or her with a conscious disregard for the rights and safety of others or ratified the conduct. With respect to a corporate employer the ratification must be on the part of an officer, director, or managing agent. Defendant has provided evidence showing that the supervisors were not managing agents and Plaintiff¿s opposing separate statement does not site to any conflicting evidence. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the failure to accommodate and failure to engage causes of action. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether Plaintiff is disabled. There is also a triable issue of fact as to whether Defendant properly accommodated and engaged in the interactive process by conditioning the accommodation on Plaintiff¿s release of her claims. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Altrnative, Issue Sanctions And/Or Evidentiary Sanctiosn And Request For Monetary Sanctions For Pltf'S Failure To Comply Wiht Order Compelling Her To Submit To Independent Medical Examinations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 4, 2011, line 3.
